vcgs.label: Unconscious contact defilement and conscience awakening
vcgs.description: Defilement incurred through contact the person may not immediately know has occurred; the guilt becomes morally real only when awareness arrives. The inner-being content is the temporal gap between defilement and recognition — conscience awakening after hidden violation. The defilement exists as an objective condition prior to any inner awareness. Cross-register: M12 (purity as the state disrupted by the unknown contact).

vcgs.label: Carcass-contact defilement
vcgs.description: The unclean state incurred by touching, carrying, or eating the carcass of an unclean or dead animal — categorically forbidden or permitted animals in a dead state. Time-bound; acquired through bodily interaction with death in animal form; resolved by washing. Inner-being content: duty of watchful restraint over bodily actions near animal carcasses; awareness that bodily actions carry ritual consequence. Extends to ingestion (Lev 17:15). Cross-register: M12.

vcgs.label: Object contamination through material transmission
vcgs.description: The unclean state transmitted from dead creatures to objects — vessels, food, water, ovens, seed, and material surfaces. Some contaminations are irreversible (earthenware). Moisture serves as a conductor of impurity. Inner-being content: duty to guard what one allows to contact provisions and utensils; the contamination field radiates from death into the material world. Cross-register: M12.

vcgs.label: Volitional self-defilement
vcgs.description: Defilement through swarming creatures framed explicitly as volitional self-corruption — the person urged to refuse contact, making the choice a matter of the will. Divine holiness is the explicit ground: the will's abstention from defiling contact is tied to imitating the holy character of God. Inner-being content: the will's capacity to choose or refuse defilement; its connection to participation in divine holiness. Cross-register: M12 (holiness as the positive state the will orients toward).

vcgs.label: Menstrual and physiological defilement cycle
vcgs.description: The ritual unclean state arising from menstruation and childbirth — physiological in origin, not moral fault, time-bound, governed by the bodily reproductive and discharge cycle. The niddah condition is the paradigm case: the state radiates outward to all surfaces contacted; transmissible through sexual contact. Childbirth and abnormal extended discharge operate by the same framework. Inner-being content: navigation of a recurring bodily state creating ritual separateness tied to the body's created processes. Cross-register: M12 (the purity cycle — restored state at end of each niddah period).

vcgs.label: Bodily discharge contact defilement
vcgs.description: The unclean state produced by bodily discharge (genital discharge / zav condition) and its transmission to surfaces, objects, and persons through contact, indirect contact, spittle, and unwashed hands. Seminal emission and sexual intercourse with emission produce a similar temporary unclean state. Inner-being content: contamination radiating from the body outward through physical interaction; duty of bodily watchfulness; moral obligation to prevent spreading one's own condition to others. Cross-register: M12 (purity restored by washing and waiting).

vcgs.label: Corpse-contact defilement
vcgs.description: The most severe sub-register — contact with a human corpse produces a seven-day unclean state requiring red-heifer ash (water of impurity) applied on third and seventh days. Defilement transmits through direct touch, carrying, spatial proximity (tent), and field contact with bones or graves. It extends to objects and cascades through contact chains. Willful refusal to cleanse constitutes defiling the sanctuary and results in excision. Inner-being content: grief at exclusion from worship (Num 9:7); weight of unresolved unclean state; moral fault of will choosing to remain defiled (Num 19:20); communal responsibility not to spread death-defilement into sacred space. Cross-register: M12 (purity restored through prescribed cleansing rite); M11 (cleansing as appointed response).

vcgs.label: Sacred boundary and defilement scope
vcgs.description: Verses that frame the scope, limits, and existential stakes of bodily defilement — uncleanness restricts sacred not secular eating; unchecked defilement is existentially dangerous to the divine dwelling; impurity has accumulated as tangible presence in sacred space; land becomes saturated with contagious defilement of surrounding peoples. Inner-being content: conscience oriented to the sacred domain; understanding the defiled/clean boundary as the boundary between ordinary life and access to holiness. Cross-register: M12 (purity required for sacred participation); M11 (cleansing of accumulated sacral contamination — 2Ch 29:5).

vcgs.label: Discernment discipline
vcgs.description: The cultivation of active moral-spiritual discernment as the inner-being faculty required for distinguishing clean from unclean — a perceptual and volitional discipline central to covenant life. Priests are called to this as their vocational responsibility; the law's purpose is framed as producing this faculty in the community. The clean/unclean distinction is a moral-identity boundary. Personal integrity is evidenced by maintaining freedom from uncleanness at the time of sacred giving. Cross-register: M12 (purity/discernment as the positive faculty the characteristic is defined against).

vcgs.label: Priestly diagnosis and disease verdict
vcgs.description: The unclean state determined by authoritative priestly examination of skin disease. The priest observes bodily markers, applies diagnostic criteria, and pronounces verdict. The verdict may be immediate or time-delayed pending spread confirmation. Progressive worsening escalates and confirms impurity status. Inner-being content: experience of bodily deterioration determining ritual standing through external authority's judgment; the person's state of body as evidence on which social and religious identity rests. Cross-register: M12 (purity as the cleared state following positive examination).

vcgs.label: Public declaration, isolation, and stigma
vcgs.description: The experience of the declared unclean person — required to publicly cry out their own contaminated state, compelled to dwell alone, physically separated from community for the full disease duration. The inner reality of contamination is verbalized outward through ritual cry, isolating the self. Extends to institutional enforcement (gatekeepers barring the unclean — 2Ch 23:19) and existential reflection that the unclean and clean share the same mortal fate (Ecc 9:2). Cross-register: M07 (stigma, disgrace, and enforced social shame); M12 (purity as the condition of communal belonging).

vcgs.label: Disease in objects and structures
vcgs.description: The categorical unclean state applied to material objects — fabric with spreading disease burned; diseased house stones removed to unclean disposal zone; plaster scraped and deposited outside the city; a house where disease persists after repair is declared persistently unclean and demolished. Entering a shut-up diseased house transmits defilement by proximity. The categorical-contamination determination and discipline of removing contamination from inhabited space reflects the same discernment-and-protective discipline as the personal disease verdict. Cross-register: M12 (clean determination the examination aims to produce for material objects).

vcgs.label: NT categorical conscience and covenantal status
vcgs.description: The NT register where categorical clean/unclean convictions are formed in conscience, maintained against challenge, and revised by divine revelation — and where clean/unclean status is extended to persons and relationships as a covenantal category. Peter's deeply formed conviction resists divine vision (Act 10:14, 11:8) until divine revelation overturns the humanly constructed inner judgment (Act 10:28). Children of a believer considered holy rather than unclean on the basis of sanctifying covenantal influence (1Cor 7:14). Categorical avoidance by those nurturing one set apart for God (Judg 13:4). Cross-register: M12 (purity/holiness as the covenantal status the boundary navigates).

vcgs.label: Constitutional and existential moral uncleanness
vcgs.description: Verses addressing the human condition itself as inherently compromised before God — the impossibility of clean from unclean (Job 14:4); the prophet's self-aware moral collapse under divine holiness (Isa 6:5); corporate confession of total defilement invalidating moral performance (Isa 64:6); community's inner moral state corrupting every act of worship (Hag 2:14). Inner-being content: recognition of defilement so deep and pervasive it characterises the inner person's constitution before God, not merely incurred by particular acts. Cross-register: M12 (divine holiness as the confronting force revealing inner defilement).

vcgs.label: Impurity as flesh-dominion and habitual corruption
vcgs.description: Impurity functioning as a prior master, slavery, or dominion over the will and bodily members — the condition to which unchecked heart-lust leads (Rom 1:24); impurity as prior master directing members toward escalating moral disorder (Rom 6:19); morally chosen, persistent, unrepented through a hardened will (2Cor 12:21); visible work of the flesh (Gal 5:19); callousness of conscience enabling greedy pursuit of sensuality (Eph 4:19); earthly inner drives to be mortified (Col 3:5); impurity as what divine calling opposes (1Th 4:7); corrupted motivation (1Th 2:3). Cross-register: M10 (sin-act producing the impurity-state); M29 (unchecked desire as vehicle of dominion).

vcgs.label: Conscience and will as locus of defilement; deliberate self-purification
vcgs.description: Verses where the conscience or will is the named primary locus of defilement, or where deliberate self-purification from defilement penetrating the full inner-outer constitution is called for. Conscience defiled by acting against its own conviction (1Cor 8:7). Defilement penetrating body and spirit requiring active self-purification (2Cor 7:1). Passion-driven defilement of will with contempt for authority (2Pe 2:10). Deliberate will called to separate from unclean things as condition for divine welcome (2Cor 6:17) and to purify before bearing sacred vessels (Isa 52:11). Cross-register: M11 (cleansing as the active response called for); M08 (pride and contempt as the will's posture — 2Pe 2:10); M12 (holiness as goal of purification).

vcgs.label: Heart allegiance disorder and idolatrous impurity
vcgs.description: The heart-allegiance dimension of moral-inner defilement — impurity equated with idolatry, arising from a heart that has displaced God with corrupt desire. Inner life full of impurity despite outward appearances (Mat 23:27). Impurity equals idolatry as heart allegiance disorder disqualifying from divine inheritance (Eph 5:5). Impurity incompatible with holy inner identity (Eph 5:3). The Revelation cosmic extension: three unclean spirits whose inner character is morally defiled, functioning as agents of deception (Rev 16:13); soul saturated with moral filth masked by seductive beauty (Rev 17:4); total spiritual and moral uncleanliness as the defining character of total corruption (Rev 18:2). Cross-register: M12 (holiness as the inner identity idolatrous impurity violates); M10 (idolatry as sin-act producing heart-defilement state).

vcgs.label: Maintained moral purity and consecrated non-defilement
vcgs.description: The positive-inverse register — the inner-being condition of those who have not defiled themselves, maintained through consecrated devotion and moral integrity. The few who have not soiled their garments have kept inner and outward life undefiled amid a compromised community; moral integrity is the basis of worthiness to walk with Christ (Rev 3:4). Total consecration to the Lamb with self-denial and devotion as the defining inner posture (Rev 14:4). Moral purity as the condition of access to the holy Way (Isa 35:8). Cross-register: M09 (humility/consecration — Rev 14:4's self-denial); M12 (purity as the positive state embodied).

vcgs.label: Sexual and covenantal-relational defilement
vcgs.description: The defilement-state produced by sexual violations of covenantal bonds — adultery, bestiality, violation of a person, transgressive marriage, and the ordeal-of-jealousy law. Inner-being content: the will's moral defection in sexual terms producing an objective defilement-state that exists independently of human detection, carries objective bodily consequences, and operates across generations. The violation of the nearest relational bonds is the shared feature. Niddah boundary violation included where treated as moral covenant transgression. Cross-register: M12 (purity as covenant fidelity); M10 (sin-act of sexual transgression).

vcgs.label: Idolatrous self-defilement
vcgs.description: The defilement-state produced by the will's pursuit of idolatrous attachment — habitual devotion to false gods, spiritual whoredom with the nations, child sacrifice, and deliberate installation of abominations in the place of worship. Inner-being content: the will's self-directed corruption through idolatrous pursuit; desire redirected toward idols; persistent self-defilement closing relational access with God; the city's deliberate corporate self-defilement. The reforming king's intentional defilement of idolatrous sites (2Ki 23 — inverse register). Idolatrous wealth and ornament becoming sources of defilement (Eze 7:19-20). Cross-register: M10b (idolatry as abomination-register); M10 (sin-act of idolatry); M29 (desire as vehicle); M12 (holiness abandoned).

vcgs.label: Sanctuary and sacred-space defilement
vcgs.description: Defilement inflicted on or threatening the sanctuary — by enemy invasion, by deliberate installation of idols and abominations in God's house, by corporate leadership's habitual defection, by divine judgment using defilement as instrument, by compound sanctuary and Sabbath desecration. The promise that in the restored order the holy name will no longer be defiled through persistent unfaithfulness (Eze 43:7-8). Priestly vocational purity guarding the sacred space by avoiding contact with death (Eze 44:25). Cross-register: M12 (holiness of the sacred space); M10 (sin-act of idolatrous installation); M10b (abomination as the quality of what is installed).

vcgs.label: Land and communal-space defilement
vcgs.description: Defilement extending from persons or community to the land itself — the moral-sacral pollution of shared inhabitable space through sin, bloodshed, and persistent moral defection. The land must not be defiled because God dwells among the people in it. Habitual conduct defiled the land; their ways as morally repugnant as menstrual impurity before God (Eze 36:17 × 2 terms). Disconnection from covenant relationship makes sustenance spiritually contaminating. Inner-being content: the community's moral disposition expressed outwardly in persistent corrupting behaviour rendering space incompatible with divine habitation. Cross-register: M12 (purity/holiness of the land); M10 (sin-act generating land-defilement).

vcgs.label: Exile, judgment consequences, and corporate cleansing promise
vcgs.description: The consequences of corporate defilement projected into the physical and social condition of the people — expulsion, exile, judgment — and the promise of divine cleansing reversing the defilement-state. Nazirite consecration nullified by corpse-proximity (Num 6:7/9/12). Jerusalem designated a filthy thing (Lam 1:17). Exiles cried 'Unclean! Away!' — total relational severance (Lam 4:15). Exile as divinely ordained consequence of prior defilement (Eze 4:13). Memory of defilement producing self-loathing as inner mechanism of repentance (Eze 20:43 — in VCG-02). Promise of no longer defiling through idols (Eze 37:23). Cleansing fountain (Zec 13:1). Failure of priestly discernment leadership (Eze 22:26). Cross-register: M11 (cleansing as promised response — Eze 37:23, Zec 13:1); M07 (corporate shame/expulsion — Lam 1:17, Lam 4:15, Eze 22:5); M12 (purity as the restored state aimed at).

vcgs.label: Inhabitation and affliction: the possessed person's defilement-state
vcgs.description: The inner-being condition of the person inhabited and dominated by an unclean spirit — will overridden, bodily agency lost, faculties suppressed, normal communal life severed. The unclean spirit's presence constitutes the person's defilement: their inner state is defined by the corrupting spiritual presence within them. Suppression of speech and hearing shows the spirit impairs inner and bodily faculties. Persistent, irresistible control illustrates total domination of the person's will and bodily agency. Cross-register: M12 (purity/wholeness as the state the inhabited person has lost); M27 (the unclean spirit as cosmic-evil agent — its character belongs to M27; the person's defilement-state belongs to M10c-E).

vcgs.label: Expulsion, resistance, and restoration of wholeness
vcgs.description: The expulsion event and its dynamics — violent departure through convulsion and outcry, destructive transfer, vocal resistance at removal from many persons, the spirit's restless drive to re-inhabit after expulsion, and crucially restoration: being freed from an unclean spirit is equated with a restoration of wholeness — spiritual defilement treated as a form of affliction that healing reverses. The accusation that Jesus's power derives from an unclean spirit addresses the expulsion-authority context. Inner-being content: the dynamics of the defilement-condition (resistance to removal, radiating destructive force) and the restorative act of healing. Cross-register: M12 (wholeness as what healing restores); M11 (healing as cleansing/restoration response).

vcgs.label: Authority over unclean spirits
vcgs.description: The commissioning of authority over unclean spirits and the spirits' recognition of divine power. Unclean spirits are hostile, morally corrupting powers over which authority is granted (Mat 10:1, Mar 6:7). The spirits' obedience reveals they are subject to a higher power — defiling agents under authoritative restraint (Mar 1:27). Unclean spirits recognise and confess a superior divine identity before being commanded (Mar 3:11). Unclean spiritual forces yield to authoritative speech, revealing defilement as ultimately subject to divine power (Luk 4:36). Cross-register: M12 (divine purity/power as the force to which defiling spirits submit); M27 (the spirits' recognition of divine authority as an M27-register event).
